ref: Rachel said, “I've struggled hard with my sister, and I've won!” So she named the boy Naphtali.
text: Rachɛl kaa dau u sɔŋ, Naftali. U kaa ŋɔ, “N ni n kpɛɛma ra naau dikuu biizi wia. Ka n tamɔɔ lugɔ minuu jaa ra tɔ see ani biizi lulaa.”
translated: Rachel bore her son, Naphtali. She said, “I have had great struggles with my rival over sons. And I have prevailed in the struggle with her, and also with another son.

ref: When they had another son,
text: Denii Zilpa kaa lul biibaal tɛɛ Jekɔb.
translated: Dinah and Zilpah bore Jacob's children.

ref: After Joseph was born, Jacob said to Laban, “Release me from our agreement and let me return to my own country.
text: Rachɛl n wa lul Joosɛf la, Jekɔb ŋɔ Laban be, “Taŋ ŋmɛnɛɛ ń bir kaali n bol.
translated: When Rachel gave birth to Joseph, Jacob said to Laban, "Let me return to my home today.

ref: Some of the sheep that Jacob was keeping for Laban were already spotted. And when the others were ready to mate, he made sure that they faced in the direction of the spotted and black ones. In this way, Jacob built up a flock of sheep for himself and did not put them with the other sheep.
text: Jekɔb ɔɔ bargi peeza la di lii buuna la ni, di gil a siwee chaŋ Laban hora la n lɛgzɛɛ di biri la. Gunnɛ u ɛɛ di na u himmaa peeza, di lii Laban hora biini.
translated: Jacob then took the livestock and went to the well, and watered Laban's flock and returned home. When he had finished watering his own livestock, he went to Laban's flock again.

ref: years to buy your sheep and goats. During that time you kept changing my wages.
text: Ií zum gunla miŋ hummɛ ni n fɔŋŋa maanii,
translated: I want to go and return, then I will call you.

ref: and I am the God you worshiped at Bethel, when you poured olive oil on a rock and made a promise to me. Leave here at once and return to the land where you were born.”
text: Minuu Kɔraawiizi la n faa ba i be Bɛtɛl, bɔr la hiŋ ɛɛ ɔlivi nuu di waari bii bɛl ni, ka ŋɔ nua tɛŋ labuu. Awia, ɛɛ sirya di bir kaali hɛri la baŋ lulii la.’ ”
translated: When Korah's father came to Bethel, he planted olive trees around the house and also planted a garden. And now, all the children who were born there.

ref: After searching through everything I have, did you find anything of yours? If so, put it here, where your relatives and mine can see it. Then we can decide what to do.
text: Hiŋ binni n kuna maa biini, bɛɛ i na u ɛɛ i horei? Kunla hiŋ nawɛɛ u ɛɛ i kuŋ, kpau lii yaa lulla naa maa see bá na, di zum unla hor n summɛ.
translated: This morning I came to meet you, but you were not there. If I had known you were not there, I would have gone to the market to buy something, so that I could come back and cook.

ref: Laban said to Jacob, “Leah and Rachel are my daughters, and their children belong to me. All these sheep you are taking are really mine too. In fact, everything you have belongs to me. But there is nothing I can do to keep my daughters and their children.
text: Laban n wa nuu guŋ, u ŋɔ Jekɔb be, “Haana naa ɛɛ n biihaanaa, biizi naa ge ɛɛ n biizi, sela naa ɛɛ n sela. Kuna naa maa hiŋ dɔɔwɛ naa ɛɛ n horaa. Ka ɛrɛɛ ń ɛɛ zinaa, dikuu n biihaana naa ni biizi la baŋ lullɛ wiai?
translated: Laban heard a rumor and told Jacob, "My daughters are my daughters, my children are my children, my sheep are my sheep. All that you see is mine. And now,

ref: he told his men to get some more rocks and pile them up next to it. Then Jacob and Laban ate a meal together beside the rocks.
text: ka ŋɔ u lulla be, “Ií chaali bia dau kululu.” Baŋ wa ɛɛ guŋ tuŋ, Jekɔb ni Laban maa so a bia zɛɛ di di.
translated: And he said to his father, “You will not pursue us again.” When they finished speaking, Jacob and Laban then made a covenant between them.

ref: Jacob told them to say to Esau, “Master, I am your servant! I have lived with Laban all this time,
text: Denii u ŋɔ ba be, “Ií la ga ŋɔ n dɔɔri Ɛsau be, dí u balii Jekɔb la n du u naara Laban be di birgi la ra jaamuu werge maa.
translated: Then he said to them, "If you are going to kill Esau, and his brother Jacob, and his uncle Laban, then the whole family will be destroyed.

ref: Jacob also told the men in charge of the second and third herds and those who followed to say the same thing when they met Esau.
text: Gunnɛ Jekɔb faa ŋɔ tɛɛ a senɔɔna sugesugezi la maa di wa kaali, di karfi baa lugɔ di ba wa chem Ɛsau ni, ba maa ŋɔ wikpaŋ la uŋ ŋɔwɛɛ la tɛu.
translated: Jacob's mother took his possessions and his young children, and they went to a far place, and they stayed there so that they would not meet Esau, and they would not see

ref: The man said, “From now on, your name will no longer be Jacob. You will be called Israel, because you have wrestled with God and with men, and you have won.”
text: A baal ŋɔ, “Filaŋkeŋ i sɔŋ waa kaa di Jekɔb. Ba daŋ jaa ra yirii Izrɛl, dikuubɛɛwiai, i ni Kɔraawiizi ani baala yoowoo, ka ba waa wette bá kpeglii.”
translated: A man said, "Pharaoh helped them to fight Jacob. They had already named him Israel, the chief, and Korah and other men, so they would come and kill him.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Jekɔb ni u diahina maa n kaa kpa ŋmɛnɛɛ di ra kaali la, Kɔraawiizi mɛlikazi wa chem ba ni.
translated: Jacob and his brothers will not be able to do anything today if they come, Korawizi's people will kill them.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Jekɔb n wa na baa u ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi ni u mɛlikazi chɔɔllɛ keŋ!” U faa dau de sɔŋ Mahanam.
translated: Jacob then said to his father, "The crow and the dove are very beautiful!" His father's name was Mahanam.

ref: Jacob got up in the middle of the night and took his wives, his eleven children, and everything he owned across to the other side of the Jabbok River for safety.
text: Uŋ wa gillɛ ba gi a muu la, u gil ba jaa paa u kuna maa ge di gi.
translated: I will go and give him water, and he went and brought it for him to come and eat.

ref: Then the man asked, “What is your name?” “Jacob,” he answered.
text: Denii a baal pɛɛzuu, “I sɔnnɛ ɛrɛɛ?” U ŋɔ, “N sɔnnɛ Jekɔb.”
translated: Dennis asked his father, "What is your name?" He said, "My name is Jacob.

ref: When the messengers returned, they told Jacob, “We went to your brother Esau, and now he is heading this way with
text: A huŋhunna bir ba Jekɔb be di wa ŋɔ, “Yaa la i kpɛɛma Ɛsau benii. U ra bau di kpogli baala kalfaanaazu bá wa chem i ni.”
translated: A messenger came to Jacob and said to him, "Look, your brother Esau is coming. He is coming with four hundred men to meet you.

ref: But Jacob answered, “Master, you know traveling is hard on children, and I have to look after the sheep and goats that are nursing their young. If my animals travel too much in one day, they will all die.
text: Denii Jekɔb bir ŋɔ u be, “N dɔɔri, n biizi naa baŋhina haa du jigzii, n peeza ni nauna gba aŋhina dɔɔ biifullii. Di i ra duŋŋaa kpegzi ni, hɔpizikpaŋ gba, a daŋ siuzi.
translated: Then Jacob's son said to him, "My father, I ask you to give me the portion of the inheritance that belongs to me, and I will go and spend it all on myself. When he had finished spending it all, a great famine also came upon him.

ref: Why don't you go on ahead and let me travel along slowly with the children, the herds, and the flocks. We can meet again in the country of Edom.”
text: Awia, tɔ see di ra la, ka ya kpogli a biizi ni a sela bɔɔiih di ra ba. Gunla maa, yaa daŋ wa huuzii Ɛdɔm.”
translated: Awia, when he returns, we will gather the children and the women who are there. On that day, we will go and attack Edom.

ref: So Esau left for Edom.
text: Awia, a hɔl hiŋ, Ɛsau kaa bir kaali Ɛdɔm.
translated: Now, that is why Esau was called Edom.

ref: Shechem was the most respected person in his family, and he was so in love with Dinah that he hurried off to get everything done.
text: Sɛkɛm hahzi manaŋ di ɛɛ gunla maa baŋ ŋɔwɛɛ la, dikuubɛɛwiai, u faa ra nyiŋŋi Dainaa ni u bampirii maa. Di faa kaa ɛɛ nɛr la u lulla maa n na tɔgzi ge.
translated: Shechem took Dinah and lay with her and disgraced her. Because she was a virgin, he loved Dinah and her family. And he spoke to her father to comfort her.

ref: Every grown man followed this advice and got circumcised. Dinah's Brothers Take Revenge
text: Denii a bolzeŋ nɛra maa sɛɛ gunla Hamɔ ni u biibaal Sɛkɛm n ŋɔwɛɛ la. A baala maa jaa teŋri ba hɛna.
translated: Then a man named Hamor and his son Shechem were there. They had come to the land before them.

ref: While they were there, Rebekah's personal servant Deborah died. They buried her under an oak tree and called it “Weeping Oak.” God Blesses Jacob at Bethel
text: Too, baŋ du de la, Dɛbɔra la n dɛɛzi Ribɛka la siu. Denii ba kpau hug daazeŋ bɛl zɛɛ n du Bɛtɛl wiifurii nodig wiikakag. Awia, ba faa dau a daa sɔŋ Alɔn Bakuf.
translated: Then, when they arrived there, Deborah, Rebecca's nurse, died. Then they buried her under the weeping oak that is near Bethel. So, they named that tree Allon Bacuth

ref: Jacob set up a tombstone over her grave, and it is still there.
text: Jekɔb faa kpa bii di baŋ u ŋmam hiŋ. U haa baŋ de haali ni zinaa.
translated: Jacob went to the farm and saw his mother there. He gave her some food and water.

ref: Esau and his three wives had five sons while in Canaan. Adah's son was Eliphaz; Basemath's son was Reuel; Oholibamah's three sons were Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
text: Ɛsau ni Ɔhɔlibama ge lul Jɛyus, Jalam ani Kɔra. Keenan hɛrii Ɛsau faa lul biibaala naa maa.
translated: Esau and Oholibamah bore Jeush, Jalam and Korah. Esau also had these children with Keenan.

ref: Korah, Gatam, and Amalek. These and Esau's other descendants lived in the land of Edom.
text: Kɔra, Gatam ani Amalɛk. Koora naabuu faa lii Ɛlifaz naanna biini, Ɛdɔm hɛri. Ba faa ɛɛ Ada naannaa.
translated: Kora, Gatam and Amalek. Kora's firstborn was Eliphaz's son, Edom's brother. They were not Ada's sons.

ref: All of these clans descended from Esau, who was known as Edom.
text: Bannii naa maa ɛɛ Ɛsau la banna yir Ɛdɔm la deere.
translated: The people of Esau and the land of Edom.

ref: Jacob loved Joseph more than he did any of his other sons, because Joseph was born when Jacob was very old. Jacob had even given Joseph a fancy coat
text: Jekɔb faa nɔŋŋuubuu di kaali u kpɛɛnna la maa, dikuu uŋ faa zɛŋŋɛ ka haa luluu la wia. Denii u kpa tagtajannanii bɛl di tɛu.
translated: Jacob took a small bird and killed it for his wife, then he took another and gave it to the child to eat. And then he went to the market to buy and sell.

text: U faa bagli u deeza hiŋ waarɛ di tɛɛ u mɛɛ gba. U mɛɛ hau u ni ka pɛɛzuu, “Beedee gunnɛi? I ra bagluu lugɔ n ni i maa ani i kpɛɛnna daŋ ŋmɛnɛ wa tognuu di ra jaamiii?”
translated: He went to the farm and stayed there for four days before he returned home. When he returned, he asked, “What happened? You went to the farm and left your child alone for eight days and they died?

translated: And it came to pass, Judah went up to shear his sheep. When he was there, Judah and his friend Adullamite Hirah went to Timnah, where he had his sheep shearers.
